case_title: IN RE: UNITED ASSOCIATION OF JOURNEYMAN AND APPRENTICES
cause_of_action: OTHER CIVIL PETITIONS (verified petition for order compelling arbitration award)

docket_entries.description: MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF VERIFIED PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION AWARD FILED BY PETITIONER UNITED ASSOCIATION OF JOURNEYMAN AND APPRENTICES OF THE PLUMBING AND PIPEFITTING INDUSTRIE, LOCAL 38, AFL-CIO

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER UNITED ASSOCIATION OF JOURNEYMAN AND APPRENTICES' HEARING ON PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION IS CONTINUED FROM SEP-12-2012 TO SEP-24-2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES.
OPPOSITION TO PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER UNITED ASSOCIATION OF JOURNEYMAN AND APPRENTICES' PETITION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE MOU CONTAINS AN ENFORCEABLE AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE THAT ENCOMPASSES THE UNDERLYING DISPUTE. ALTHOUGH THE MOU HAS NOT BEEN SUBMITTED IN A MANNER THAT IS ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE, RESPONDENT DOES NOT DISPUTE THAT THE MOU IS THE GOVERNING AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES. PETITIONER NEED NOT, AT THIS JUNCTURE, PRESENT EVIDENCE SUPPORTING ITS UNDERLYING CLAIM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077.
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-24-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-24-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICHAEL SEVILLE, ESQ., SEVILLE LAW FIRM, 415.722.0837, FOR PETITIONER UNITED ASSOCIATION OF JOURNEYMAN AND APPRENTICES.
RAFAL OFIERSKI, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 415.554.3800, FOR DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTOF SAN

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER UNITED ASSOCIATION OF JOURNEYMAN AND APPRENTICES' Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration is granted. The MOU contains an enforceable agreement to arbitrate that encompasses the underlying dispute. Although the MOU has not been submitted in a manner that is admissible evidence, Respondent does not dispute that the MOU is the governing agreement between the parties. Petitioner need not, at this juncture, present evidence supporting its underlying claim. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Aug-29-2012 Continued To Sep-24-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
